seo.title: Building Code Generation with Agno + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with Agno + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Agno (formerly Phidata) is a lightweight, model-agnostic framework for building multi-modal AI agents, perfect for code generation workflows that require sandbox execution and iterative refinement. Guardrails are essential because code generation carries high risks like credential leakage, unsafe execution, and syntax errors that can lead to production failures or security breaches. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure your Agno agents generate safe, valid, and dependency-secure code.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with Agno
architectureOverview.description:
The typical Agno code generation architecture starts with an LLM agent receiving a coding task, generating initial code using structured tools. The code is executed in a sandboxed environment (like E2B or Docker), with results fed back for validation against test cases. If tests fail, the agent iteratively refines the code through multiple rounds, maintaining conversation memory for context.

Agno's tool system enables seamless integration of sandbox execution, test runners, and linters. The agent maintains state across iterations, allowing sophisticated refinement loops. Vex verification wraps each agent.run() call to catch safety issues before execution.

This creates a robust feedback loop: Generate → Sandbox Execute → Test → Verify → Refine, with Vex ensuring safety at every step.

risks.name: Malicious Code Injection
risks.description: Agent generates code with system calls, file deletions, or network requests that could harm infrastructure
risks.mitigation: Vex scans for dangerous APIs and blocks execution before sandbox

risks.name: Credential Leakage
risks.description: Generated code embeds API keys, passwords, or secrets from agent context or memory
risks.mitigation: Vex regex patterns detect and redact credentials in generated code

risks.name: Unsafe Dependencies
risks.description: Agent installs malicious packages or uses vulnerable dependency versions
risks.mitigation: Vex dependency scanner blocks known malicious packages

risks.name: Infinite Execution Loops
risks.description: Code contains while(True) loops or recursive calls without termination
risks.mitigation: Vex runtime monitoring terminates executions exceeding time/memory limits

implementation.title: Basic Agno Code Generation Agent
implementation.description: Create a simple Agno agent with code generation and sandbox tools
implementation.code:
from agno.agent import Agent
from agno.tools.python import PythonTool

agent = Agent(
    name="CodeGenAgent",
    model="anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet-latest",
    tools=[PythonTool()],
    instructions="""You are a code generation expert. Generate clean, tested Python code.
    Always include comprehensive test cases."""
)

result = agent.run("Write a function to validate emails and include tests")
print(result)

implementation.title: Sandbox Execution Integration
implementation.description: Add E2B sandbox for safe code execution with test validation
implementation.code:
from agno.tools.e2b import E2BCodeInterpreter
from agno.agent import Agent

sandbox_tool = E2BCodeInterpreter(api_key="your_e2b_key")

agent = Agent(
    name="SafeCodeAgent",
    model="openai/gpt-4o",
    tools=[sandbox_tool],
    instructions="Execute code in sandbox. Run pytest on generated code."
)

result = agent.run("Create a REST API client with error handling and tests")
print(result['output'])

implementation.title: Iterative Refinement Loop
implementation.description: Implement feedback loop for code improvement based on test failures
implementation.code:
from agno.agent import Agent
from agno.tools.python import PythonTool

agent = Agent(
    name="RefineAgent",
    model="anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet-latest",
    tools=[PythonTool()],
    show_tool_calls=True
)

def refine_code(task, max_iterations=3):
    feedback = ""
    for i in range(max_iterations):
        result = agent.run(f"{task}\nPrevious feedback: {feedback}")
        test_result = result.tools[0].run_tests()
        if test_result['passed']:
            return result
        feedback = test_result['feedback']
    return result

final_code = refine_code("Build a secure JWT validator with tests")

implementation.title: Structured Code Output Schema
implementation.description: Enforce structured output with code, tests, and documentation
implementation.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from agno.agent import Agent

class CodeOutput(BaseModel):
    code: str = Field(description="Clean, executable Python code")
    tests: str = Field(description="Pytest test cases")
    docs: str = Field(description="Documentation and usage")

agent = Agent(
    name="StructuredCodeAgent",
    model="openai/gpt-4o",
    output_type=CodeOutput,
    instructions="Always return structured code with tests and docs"
)

result = agent.run("Generate a rate limiter class")
print(result.code)
print(result.tests)

vexIntegration.title: Vex Decorator on agent.run()
vexIntegration.description: Wrap Agno's agent.run() with Vex verification for code safety

vexIntegration.title: Sandbox + Vex Double Verification
vexIntegration.description: Pre-verify code before expensive sandbox execution
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import verify
from agno.tools.e2b import E2BCodeInterpreter
from agno.agent import Agent

@verify(policies=["code_safety", "syntax_valid", "no_credentials"])
def verified_code_gen(agent, task):
    result = agent.run(task)
    # Vex blocks if unsafe
    return result

sandbox = E2BCodeInterpreter()
agent = Agent(tools=[sandbox])

# Only safe code reaches expensive sandbox
safe_result = verified_code_gen(
    agent, 
    "Create a data processing pipeline with tests"
)

execution = sandbox.run(safe_result.code)
